things.name: Tel Be'er Sheva (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage ancient tell at the edge of Beer Sheva; excavations have revealed an Iron Age city with a unique horned altar and sophisticated water system from biblical times

things.name: Negev Desert
things.description: Israel's vast southern desert covers 60% of the country; Beer Sheva is the gateway to the Makhtesh Ramon crater, Nabataean cities of Avdat and the extraordinary desert landscapes

things.name: Ramon Crater (Makhtesh Ramon)
things.description: The world's largest erosion crater, 40km long and 500 metres deep; a geological wonder with a unique ecosystem; the Mitzpe Ramon visitor centre explains this extraordinary formation

things.name: Beer Sheva Old City
things.description: Ottoman-era old town with a traditional marketplace, the Abraham's Well monument and the Old City Museum; the historic core of this ancient desert crossroads city

things.name: Negev Museum of Art
things.description: Contemporary art museum in a beautifully converted Ottoman mosque in the old city with exhibitions of Israeli and international modern art; a striking cultural centrepiece

things.name: Ben Gurion University
things.description: Israel's leading university in Beer Sheva, named after Israel's founding father who chose to retire to the Negev; a vibrant campus that transformed the desert city

things.name: WWII Beersheba Cemetery
things.description: Commonwealth War Graves cemetery honouring the 1,240 Allied soldiers who fell in the Battle of Beersheba (1917); the light horse charge here was a turning point in the Middle Eastern campaign

things.name: Bedouin Market (Thursday Market)
things.description: Traditional weekly Bedouin market with camels, handcrafted Negev textiles, spices and antiques; an authentic experience of Negev Bedouin culture that has taken place for generations

things.name: Avdat Nabataean City (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Nabataean city 65km south of Beer Sheva on the Spice Route; spectacularly preserved Byzantine churches and wine presses on a dramatic hilltop

things.name: Turner Stadium
things.description: Home of Hapoel Beer Sheva with 16,000 capacity; the club's back-to-back Israeli championship seasons (2016, 2017) led to a remarkable UEFA Champions League group stage in 2016-17
